---
about:  
  template: trestles
  image: img/profile.jpg
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - text: "{{< fa brands github size=huge >}}"
      href: https://github.com/hrlai
    - text: "{{< ai google-scholar size=huge >}}"
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=g9SWxxcAAAAJ&view_op=list_works&sortby=pubdate
    - text: "{{< fa brands researchgate size=huge >}}"
      href: https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Hao_Ran_Lai
    - text: "{{< fa brands linkedin size=huge >}}"
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/haoranlai/
    - text: "{{< fa brands orcid size=huge >}}"
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0001-6871-0146
    - text: "{{< fa envelope size=huge >}}"
      href: mailto:hrlai.ecology@gmail.com
  
toc: false
---

```{r}
#| include: false
library(yaml)

pubs_all <- read_yaml("data/publications.yaml")

# Render a markdown list of publications tagged with a given theme
theme_list <- function(tag) {
  tagged <- Filter(\(p) !is.null(p$themes) && tag %in% p$themes, pubs_all)
  for (p in tagged) {
    authors <- strsplit(p$author, " and ")[[1]]
    surname <- strsplit(authors[1], ",")[[1]][1]
    suffix <- if (length(authors) > 1) " et al." else ""
    journal <- gsub("&amp;", "&", p$journal)
    cat(sprintf(
      "- %s%s (%s) [%s](%s){target='_blank'} *%s*\n\n",
      surname,
      suffix,
      p$year,
      p$title,
      p$url,
      journal
    ))
  }
}
```

## Quantitative and Functional Ecology

Apa khabar! I'm Lai Hao Ran (賴浩然) and a plant community ecologist. My research aims to understand and generalise the dynamics of forest biodiversity through functional traits, statistical rigour, and long-term monitoring. My career goal is to build capacity and foster a more innovative research community in Malaysia and tropical Asia.

Please feel free to reach out for research collaboration, forest fieldwork, guest teaching, and project (co)supervision.

## Research themes

With selected publications.

### Functional trait ecology

Plant functional traits are a universal currency for understanding how species respond to their environment and shape the communities around them. My work uses traits to explain forest community assembly, successional dynamics, and biodiversity patterns across tropical and subtropical systems — from Borneo to New Zealand.

```{r}
#| output: asis
theme_list("traits")
```

### Species interactions and coexistence

Species do not exist in isolation: competitive, facilitative, and non-additive biotic interactions jointly determine who persists and where. I develop and apply frameworks from modern coexistence theory and ecological network science to quantify these interactions and understand how they propagate through communities under disturbance.

```{r}
#| output: asis
theme_list("interactions")
```

### Quantitative methods

Ecological inference is only as good as the models behind it. I develop and apply novel statistical approaches — including hierarchical models, model-based ordination, and joint species distribution models — to extract rigorous ecological signal from observational and long-term monitoring data.

```{r}
#| output: asis
theme_list("methods")
```
